free_text: Inclusive proton reactions at 164 MeV.
Indiana University Cyclotron Facility.
. First telescope consisted
of a 100 mu-m thick Silicon Detector backed by 7.6 cm
thick Nai(TL) crystal,
which is a thickness sufficient to stop the highest
possible  energy  protons.   For particles whose energy
was measured by the Nai(TL) cristal, a correction  was
made  for the energy lost in the dead layer in front of
the cristal.
. Second telescope consisted
of three surface barrier detectors,50,1000 and 5000 mum
thick, respectively, backed by a Veto detector.
The Proton, Deutron, and Triton Spectra were corrected
for nuclear interactions in the Nai(TL) detector.
Since the Data Needed to Make this Correction
Apparently only exist for protons, the same correction
formula was used for the deutron and the triton spectra
as was used for the protons. While the interaction
cross sections for the heavier particles are greater,
their range is smaller, and therefore, this procedure
is probably acceptable. The range of the Z=2 particles
were so small that nuclear interaction corrections were
unnecessary.
.Monte Carlo Cascade Code Vegas.
*By compiler*.This paper contains the set of smoothed
spectra for different particles.   The size of these
figures  is very small.
. Transformation to lower case and some
reaction lines are corrected.

to new EXFOR rules in Subents 023-033

with Energy >30 and >96 MeV are presented too.
